sections.paragraph:
πάντοτε adv. (on the formation Schwyzer I 629) of time (Hellenist. and Mod. Gk.; Dionys. Hal.+; Peripl. Eryth. 29; Epict., Ench. 14, 1; Dio Chrys. 15 [32], 37; Herodian 3, 9, 8; Artem. 4, 20; Plut.; Athen.; Diog. L.; OGI 458, 76 [I b.c.], SIG 814, 37 [67 a.d.]; BGU 1123, 8 [I b.c.]; PGiss 17, 4; 72, 11 [II a.d.]; Wsd 11:21; 19:18; TestSol, Test12Patr; JosAs 7:6; SyrBar 12:2; Jos., Bell. 3, 42; Just., D. 49, 7; 93, 4; Ath., R. 1 p. 49, 1 al.—The Atticists preferred ἑκάστοτε, διαπαντός, or ἀεί [Phryn. 103 Lob.]) always, at all times Mt 26:11ab; Mk 14:7ab; Lk 15:31; 18:1; J 6:34; 7:6 (seven times in J); Ro 1:10; 1 Cor 1:4; 15:58; 2 Cor 2:14 (27 times in Paul); Hb 7:25 (not found in Ac and Cath. Epistles; B-D-F §105; cp. Rob. 300); Dg 11:4; IEph 4:2; Hv 1, 1, 7 (24 times in Hermas).—B. 984. DELG s.v. πᾶς. 35–41. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

Mod. Gk. Mod. Gk. = Modern Greek

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

SyrBar SyrBar = Apocalypsis Syriaca Baruch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Hermas Hermas , s. H—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 755
